input.prompt:
My OrderProcessor.cs uses DateTime.UtcNow in several places and it's impossible
to write deterministic tests. Can you find all the time-related static calls in
my project and tell me what to do about them?

data.content:
Here's what I found — **7 time-related static calls** across two files:

### `OrderProcessor.cs`
| Line | Call | Issue |
|------|------|-------|
| 21 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Sets `CreatedAt` |
| 22 | `DateTime.UtcNow.AddDays(30)` | Sets `ExpiresAt` |
| 25 | `DateTime.Now` | Receipt string (also mixes `Now` with `UtcNow` — a bug) |
| 34 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Expiry check |

### `NotificationService.cs`
| Line | Call | Issue |
|------|------|-------|
| 7 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Days-until-expiry calculation |
| 16 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Log filename |
| 18 | `DateTime.UtcNow` | Log timestamp |

### Recommendation: Inject `TimeProvider`

.NET 8+ provides the built-in [`TimeProvider`](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.timeprovider) abstract class. Replace every `DateTime.UtcNow` / `DateTime.Now` with it:

```csharp
// Add to constructors
public OrderProcessor(ILogger<OrderProcessor> logger, TimeProvider timeProvider)
{
    _timeProvider = timeProvider;
}

// Replace calls like:
CreatedAt = _timeProvider.GetUtcNow().UtcDateTime,
```

**In production**, register `TimeProvider.System` in DI. **In tests**, use `Microsoft.Extensions.Time.Testing.FakeTimeProvider` to freeze/advance time deterministically.

Also note line 25 uses `DateTime.Now` (local time) while everything else uses `UtcNow` — that's likely a bug worth fixing when you refactor.

Want me to go ahead and make these changes?
